lessons.title: Why Were We Created?
lessons.subtitle: Discovering Human Purpose through Divine Revelation & Classical Wisdom
objectives: Explain why the purpose of something is determined by its maker.
Explain why human intellect alone cannot determine the purpose of human existence.
Identify the correct source for answering the question, 'Why were we created?'
Explain the Islamic answer to the purpose of creation from both Allah's perspective and humanity's perspective.
Support their answer using Qur'anic evidence.

sections.title: 1. Introduction
sections.content:
Every human being, whether young or old, eventually asks some very important questions:

• Why am I here?
• What is the purpose of my existence?
• Is there a reason behind my creation?
• What am I supposed to do in this life?

Although these questions seem simple, they are among the most important questions a person can ask. In fact, the way a person answers these questions determines how he will live his entire life.

Before we answer these questions, let us begin with a simple example.

callout.title: Core Principle
callout.text: The way a person answers these questions determines how he will live his entire life.

sections.title: 2. A Strange Tool
sections.story:
Imagine that you are cleaning your garage, basement, or attic and you discover a strange tool. You have never seen it before and have no idea what it does. How would you find out its purpose?

You might search online. You might ask someone knowledgeable. You might look for the instruction manual. Most importantly, you would try to find the company or person who made it because the maker knows better than anyone else why the tool was designed.

Now imagine that someone tells you, 'This tool has no purpose at all. It was made for nothing.' Would that answer satisfy you? Of course not.

options.feedback: Guessing alone can lead to misunderstanding the item's true function.

options.text: Assume it has no purpose at all
options.feedback: Everything that is intentionally made has a purpose!

options.text: Find the company or person who made it
options.feedback: Exactly! The maker knows better than anyone else why the tool was designed.

sections.takeaway:
We naturally understand that tools are made for a reason. A hammer is made to drive nails. A key is made to open locks. A phone is made for communication. Everything that is intentionally made has a purpose. In fact, the value of something is closely connected to its purpose. A tool that serves an important purpose is valuable. A tool that serves no purpose is usually considered worthless.

The value of anything is determined by its purpose.

sections.title: 3. From the Tool to Yourself
sections.content:
Now let us replace the tool with something much more important: yourself.

If a simple tool has a purpose, is it possible that a human being does not? If a hammer, a phone, or a key was not created without purpose, then how could a human being, who is far more complex and valuable, exist without purpose?

Think about it carefully. Most people can easily explain the purpose of a shoe. They might say it protects the feet, helps us walk comfortably, allows us to run, and protects us from injury. Yet if you ask many people, 'What is the purpose of a human being?' they become confused and give different answers, or sometimes no answer at all.

Does that mean that a shoe has more purpose than a human being? Of course not. Rather, it means that many people have forgotten where to look for the answer.

Just as the manufacturer of the shoe knows why it was made, the Creator of mankind knows why mankind was created. Therefore, if we truly want to know our purpose, we must ask the One who created us.

sections.title: 4. A Question That Confused Great Thinkers
sections.content:
Throughout history, many philosophers and intellectuals tried to answer the question: 'Why were we created?'

Although they were highly intelligent, many of them disagreed with one another and never arrived at a clear answer. The famous philosopher Plato is reported to have said:
'I neither know nor think that I know.'

This shows us that intelligence alone is not enough to answer every question. The greatest thinkers were able to study mathematics, science, politics, and many other subjects, yet they remained confused about the purpose of human existence.
Why? Because they were searching for the answer in the wrong place.
sections.explanation:
The confusion comes from relying entirely on human intellect. The intellect is an amazing gift from Allah. It helps us learn, think, compare, analyze, and understand many things. However, the intellect has limits.

A machine cannot explain why it was created. A painting cannot explain why the artist painted it. A tool cannot explain why the manufacturer produced it. Likewise, human beings cannot independently determine the ultimate purpose of their existence because they did not create themselves. Only the Creator knows the true reason behind the creation.
sections.solution: Therefore, if we want the correct answer, we must refer to Allah. Allah has not left humanity without guidance. He revealed the Qur'an as guidance for mankind, teaching us who we are, where we came from, where we are going, and why we were created. In a sense, the Qur'an functions as the instruction manual for human life.

sections.title: 5. The Answer from the Perspective of Allah

verses.translation: Allah is the Creator of everything.
verses.note: When we say Allah is the Creator, we affirm one of His perfect attributes. Allah creates because He is the Creator, just as He provides because He is the Provider and shows mercy because He is the Most Merciful. Therefore, from the perspective pertaining to Allah, we exist because Allah created us, and He is the Creator of all things.

sections.title: 6. The Answer from the Perspective of Human Beings

verses.arabic: الَّذِي خَلَقَ الْمَوْتَ وَالْحَيَاةَ لِيَبْلُوَكُمْ أَيُّكُمْ أَحْسَنُ عَمَلًا
verses.translation: The One who created death and life to test you as to which of you is best in deeds.
verses.note: This verse teaches us that life is a test. School has tests. Sports have tryouts. Driving requires examinations. Important positions require evaluation. Likewise, this worldly life is a test. Every day we are being tested through our choices, actions, words, intentions, blessings, hardships, successes, and failures.

sections.title: 7. What Is the Test About?
sections.intro:
Allah further explains the nature of this test. He says in Surah Adh-Dhariyat (51:56): 'And I did not create the jinn and mankind except that they worship Me.'

This verse provides the direct answer to the question of our purpose. We were created to worship Allah. Many people hear the word worship and immediately think only of prayer or fasting. However, worship in Islam is much broader than that. Worship includes prayer, fasting, charity, dhikr, reading the Qur'an, honoring one's parents, speaking truthfully, helping others, seeking beneficial knowledge, fulfilling responsibilities, and every action done sincerely for the sake of Allah.

A Muslim's entire life can become an act of worship when it is lived according to Allah's guidance. Therefore, the test of life is whether we will choose to worship Allah and obey Him or whether we will follow our desires and neglect the purpose for which we were created.

sections.title: 8. Conclusion
sections.content:
Every created thing has a purpose.
The best person to explain the purpose of something is its maker.
Human beings are creations of Allah.
Therefore, the only source that can definitively explain our purpose is Allah Himself.

Allah informed us that:
• He is the Creator of everything.
• This life is a test.
• The purpose of that test is to determine who performs the best deeds.
• We were created to worship Him.

When a person understands this purpose, life begins to make sense. Success is no longer measured merely by wealth, popularity, or worldly achievements. True success is fulfilling the purpose for which Allah created us and preparing for the eternal life that awaits after this temporary world.

quiz.question: Who is best qualified to define the purpose of an object or creation?
options: Anyone who happens to find it
The creator / manufacturer of that object
It has no purpose until someone invents one
quiz.dalil: The Maker Principle: Just as a shoe manufacturer knows why a shoe was made, Allah—the Creator—knows why mankind was created.
quiz.explanation: Just as the manufacturer knows why a tool was made, Allah—the Creator—defines the purpose of human existence.

quiz.question: Why did famous philosophers like Plato struggle to agree on human purpose?
options: They relied solely on human intellect without seeking Divine Revelation
They did not care about the question
They did not have modern technology
quiz.dalil: Plato's Admission: 'I neither know nor think that I know.' Human intellect has limits and cannot guess its purpose without revelation.
quiz.explanation: Human intellect has limits. A created entity cannot determine its purpose without reference to its Creator.

quiz.question: According to Surah Adh-Dhariyat (51:56), why were jinn and mankind created?
options: To compete with one another in power
quiz.dalil: Surah Adh-Dhariyat (51:56): 'And I did not create the jinn and mankind except that they worship Me.'
quiz.explanation: Allah states clearly: 'And I did not create the jinn and mankind except that they worship Me.'

quiz.question: From the perspective of human beings, what is the nature of this worldly life according to Surah Al-Mulk (67:2)?
options: It is purely a playground without consequences
It is a test to determine who performs the best deeds
quiz.dalil: Surah Al-Mulk (67:2): 'The One who created death and life to test you as to which of you is best in deeds.'
quiz.explanation: Life is an examination ground (like school tests or sports tryouts) to evaluate our choices and actions.

lessons.title: Knowing Allah Through His Names & Attributes
lessons.subtitle: Understanding Asma wa Sifat and How They Transform the Heart
objectives: Understand why knowing Allah's Names & Attributes is foundational to Tawheed.
Differentiate between Ruboobiyyah, Uloohiyyah, and Asma wa Sifat.
Explain how knowing Allah as Ar-Rahman and Al-Alim impacts daily character.

lessons.subtitle: How Faith Is Inherent in Human Nature

lessons.title: Tawheed in Daily Life
lessons.subtitle: Living Sincere Faith in Modern Times

flashcards.definition: The innate, natural inclination within every human being to recognize and worship One Creator.
An all-inclusive term for every word, action, and intention loved and accepted by Allah.
The Oneness of Allah in His Lordship—creating, sustaining, and managing all creation.
The Oneness of Allah in Worship—directing all prayers, hopes, and devotion to Him alone.
The Oneness of Allah in His Divine Names and Attributes without likeness or distortion.
Wisdom; finding the most beneficial correct answer faithful to revelation.
